babyLearning.en: growing fast, hearing your voice more clearly now
babyLearning.hi: tezi se badh raha hai, ab aapki aawaz aur saaf sun raha hai

insight.en: The word discipline does not mean to punish — it means to teach. Your job will not be to make your child suffer, but to help them learn.
insight.hi: Anushaasan ka matlab saza dena nahi hai — iska matlab hai sikhaana. Aapka kaam apne bachche ko dukh dena nahi hoga, balki unhe seekhne mein madad karna hoga.

expanded.en: We often imagine discipline as something stern — a frown, a punishment, a consequence handed down. But at its root, to discipline simply means to teach and to guide. A child who breaks a cup or forgets a promise is not bad; they are unfinished, learning the world one mistake at a time. When you see discipline as teaching, everything softens. You stop asking 'how do I make them stop?' and start asking 'what can I help them understand?' That small shift will shape a thousand calmer moments ahead. You are not raising someone to fear you — you are raising someone to learn from you.
expanded.hi: Hum aksar anushaasan ko kisi kathor cheez ki tarah sochte hain — ek tyoodi, ek saza, ek niyam thopa hua. Par apni jad mein, anushaasan ka matlab bas sikhaana aur raah dikhaana hai. Ek bachcha jo cup todta hai ya vaada bhool jaata hai, woh bura nahi hai; woh adhoora hai, ek-ek galti se duniya seekh raha hai. Jab aap anushaasan ko sikhaane ki tarah dekhti hain, sab kuch naram ho jaata hai. Aap poochna band kar deti hain 'main inhe kaise rokoon?' aur poochne lagti hain 'main inhe kya samajhne mein madad kar sakti hoon?' Yeh chhota sa badlaav aage hazaaron shaant pal gadhega. Aap kisi ko aapse darne ke liye nahi paal rahi — aap kisi ko aapse seekhne ke liye paal rahi hain.

deepDive.en: The Latin root of discipline, 'disciplina,' means instruction and learning, the same root as 'disciple.' Child development experts find that teaching-based guidance builds far more lasting good behaviour than fear-based punishment.
deepDive.hi: Discipline ki Latin jad, 'disciplina,' ka matlab hai shiksha aur seekhna, wahi jad jo 'disciple' ki hai. Child development experts paate hain ki sikhaane par aadhaarit margdarshan dar par aadhaarit saza se kahin zyada sthayi achha vyavhaar banata hai.

remember.en: To discipline is to teach, not to punish.
remember.hi: Anushaasan dena sikhaana hai, saza dena nahi.

summary.en: A flattering fox tricks a proud crow into dropping its prize.
summary.hi: Ek chaaploos lomdi ek ghamandi kauwe ko phusla kar uska inaam giraa deti hai.

body.en:
A crow once found a fine piece of cheese and flew up to a high branch to enjoy it in peace. A hungry fox passing below saw the cheese and at once grew clever. 'Good day, dear crow,' she called sweetly. 'How glossy your feathers are, how bright your eyes! Surely a bird so beautiful must have a voice to match. Will you not sing one note for me?'

The crow, swelling with pride at such praise, quite forgot the cheese in his beak. He opened his mouth wide to sing — and the cheese tumbled straight down into the waiting fox's jaws.

'Thank you,' laughed the fox, trotting away with her prize. 'In exchange for your cheese, take this little lesson: do not trust those who flatter you, for they are after something of yours.' The crow sat in silence, wiser, if a little hungrier, than before.
body.hi:
Ek kauwe ko ek baar paneer ka achha tukda mila aur woh use shaanti se khaane ke liye ek ooonchi tehni par ud gaya. Neeche se guzarti ek bhookhi lomdi ne paneer dekha aur turant chalaak ho gayi. 'Namaste, pyaare kauwe,' usne meethe se pukaara. 'Tumhare par kitne chamkeele hain, tumhari aankhein kitni roshan! Itna sundar pakshi to zaroor utni hi sundar aawaz rakhta hoga. Kya tum mere liye ek sur nahi gaaoge?'

Itni taareef par ghamand se phoolte hue, kauwa apni chonch mein paneer bilkul bhool gaya. Usne gaane ke liye apna munh chaudaa khola — aur paneer seedha neeche intezaar karti lomdi ke munh mein gir gaya.

'Shukriya,' lomdi hansi, apna inaam leke chalti bani. 'Tumhare paneer ke badle, yeh chhoti seekh lo: un par bharosa mat karo jo tumhari chaaploosi karte hain, kyunki woh tumhari kisi cheez ke peeche hote hain.' Kauwa chup baitha raha, pehle se zyada samajhdaar, bhale hi thoda zyada bhookha.

title.en: Tell your baby that it is okay to make mistakes — that getting things wrong is just part of learning.
title.hi: Apne baby ko bataayein ki galtiyaan karna theek hai — ki cheezein galat karna bas seekhne ka hissa hai.

motivation.en: A child who isn't afraid of mistakes grows brave enough to try.
motivation.hi: Jo bachcha galtiyon se nahi darta woh koshish karne layak himmati ban jaata hai.

description.en: Morning raga for a bright, gentle start
description.hi: Subah ka raag, ek roshan, naram shuruaat ke liye

affirmation.en: As this morning melody swings gently, so does my heart, lightly and warmly, towards you.
affirmation.hi: Jaise yeh subah ki dhun pyaar se jhoolti hai, waise hi mera dil, halke aur garm, tumhari taraf jhoolta hai.

about.en: Ragas are classical Indian melodies, each tied to a mood and time of day. From about week 18 your baby can hear; even now, the calm a raga brings you surrounds your baby and gently shapes a peaceful environment around your pregnancy.
about.hi: Raag classical Bharatiya dhunein hain, har ek kisi mood aur din ke samay se judi. Lagbhag 18ve hafte se aapka baby sun sakta hai; abhi bhi, raag se aane wali shaanti aapke baby ko gher leti hai aur aapki pregnancy ke around ek shaant mahaul banati hai.

howToUse.en: Play the raga softly while you sit or rest with a hand on your belly. You needn't focus hard — let the sound fill the room as you breathe slowly. Two to five minutes, ideally at the raga's time of day, makes it a ritual your baby will one day recognise.
howToUse.hi: Raag dheere se chalaayein jab aap baithein ya pet par haath rakh kar aaram karein. Zyada dhyan lagane ki zaroorat nahi — aawaz ko kamre mein bhar jaane dein jab aap dheere saans lein. Do se paanch minute, ho sake to raag ke samay par, ise ek aisa ritual bana deta hai jise aapka baby ek din pehchaanega.

title.en: I Am Learning As I Go

content.en: I do not need to have parenting figured out today. I am allowed to learn it slowly, the same way I will let my child learn.
content.hi: Mujhe aaj parenting samajh leni zaroori nahi. Mujhe ise dheere-dheere seekhne ki ijaazat hai, usi tarah jaise main apne bachche ko seekhne dungi.

remember.en: You can learn the same way you'll let your child learn.
remember.hi: Aap usi tarah seekh sakti hain jaise aap apne bachche ko seekhne dengi.

babyLearning.en: growing stronger, beginning to recognise familiar sounds
babyLearning.hi: aur mazboot badh raha hai, jaani-pehchaani aawazein pehchaanne lagaa

title.en: Behind Every Behaviour Is A Need
title.hi: Har Vyavhaar Ke Peeche Ek Zaroorat Hai

insight.en: Children rarely act out for no reason. Behind almost every difficult behaviour is an unmet need quietly asking to be seen.
insight.hi: Bachche shaayad hi bina kisi kaaran ke utpaat karte hain. Lagbhag har mushkil vyavhaar ke peeche ek adhoori zaroorat hoti hai jo chupchaap dekhe jaane ko maangti hai.

expanded.en: A tantrum, a refusal, a sudden meltdown — these can look like defiance, but they are usually a signal. A tired, hungry, overwhelmed or lonely child does not have the words to say so, so the feeling spills out as behaviour instead. When you learn to ask 'what does this child need right now?' instead of 'why are they being difficult?' you become a detective of love rather than a judge. The behaviour is the smoke; the need is the fire. Tend to the need, and the behaviour very often settles on its own. This way of seeing will carry you through countless hard moments ahead.
expanded.hi: Ek tantrum, ek inkaar, ek achaanak rona — yeh avgya jaisa lag sakta hai, par yeh aksar ek sanket hota hai. Ek thaka, bhookha, pareshaan ya akela bachcha ise kehne ke shabd nahi rakhta, isliye bhaavna vyavhaar ban kar baahar nikal aati hai. Jab aap poochna seekhti hain 'is bachche ko abhi kya chahiye?' bajaaye 'yeh itna mushkil kyun ho raha hai?' to aap ek judge ke bajaaye pyaar ki jasoos ban jaati hain. Vyavhaar dhuaan hai; zaroorat aag hai. Zaroorat ka khayal rakhein, aur vyavhaar aksar khud-ba-khud shaant ho jaata hai. Dekhne ka yeh tareeka aapko aage anginat mushkil palon se paar le jayega.

deepDive.en: Child psychologists describe difficult behaviour as 'communication, not manipulation.' A young brain under stress cannot reason or self-soothe well, so the calmest adult in the room becomes the child's missing brakes.
deepDive.hi: Child psychologists mushkil vyavhaar ko 'communication, manipulation nahi' bataate hain. Tanaav mein ek nanha dimaag achhi tarah soch ya khud ko shaant nahi kar paata, isliye kamre mein sabse shaant bada hi bachche ke gum hue brake ban jaata hai.

remember.en: Look for the need behind the behaviour.
remember.hi: Vyavhaar ke peeche ki zaroorat dhoondein.

summary.en: A meddling monkey learns that not every task is his to finish.
summary.hi: Ek taang adaane wala bandar seekhta hai ki har kaam use poora karne ke liye nahi hota.

body.en:
Some carpenters were building a temple in a forest clearing. At midday they went off to eat, leaving a half-sawn log with a wedge driven into the gap to hold the cut open. A troop of monkeys came swinging down to play among the timber and tools.

One curious monkey spotted the wedge sticking out of the log and decided he simply must pull it loose. He sat astride the log, gripped the wedge with both hands, and tugged at it with all his might, paying no mind to what it was for.

With a sudden crack the wedge flew out — and the gap snapped shut on the monkey's tail, trapping him fast. He howled and struggled, but it was too late. The old tale ends with a gentle smile: those who meddle in work they do not understand often end up caught by it.
body.hi:
Kuch barhai ek jungle ke maidaan mein ek mandir bana rahe the. Dopahar ko woh khaana khaane chale gaye, ek aadhe chire hue lattha chhod kar jismein katai khuli rakhne ke liye ek khoonta thoka hua tha. Bandaron ka ek jhund lakdi aur auzaaron ke beech khelne ke liye jhoolta hua aa gaya.

Ek jigyaasu bandar ne lattha se nikla khoonta dekha aur tay kiya ki use to bas use kheench kar nikaalna hi hai. Woh lattha par taang phaila kar baith gaya, dono haathon se khoonta pakda, aur poori taakat se kheencha, yeh dhyan diye bina ki woh kis liye tha.

Ek achaanak chatak ke saath khoonta nikal gaya — aur khaali jagah bandar ki poonchh par band ho gayi, use mazbooti se phasaa kar. Woh chillaaya aur chhootne ki koshish ki, par der ho chuki thi. Purani kahani ek naram muskaan ke saath khatam hoti hai: jo us kaam mein taang adaate hain jise woh samajhte nahi, woh aksar usi mein phans jaate hain.

title.en: Tell your baby that you love them completely — not for what they do, but simply for who they are.
title.hi: Apne baby ko bataayein ki aap unhe poori tarah pyaar karti hain — woh kya karte hain iske liye nahi, balki bas iske liye ki woh kaun hain.

motivation.en: Love that asks for nothing back is the safest ground a child can stand on.
motivation.hi: Jo pyaar badle mein kuch nahi maangta woh sabse surakshit zameen hai jis par bachcha khada ho sakta hai.

title.hi: Main Satah Ke Neeche Sunti Hoon

affirmation.en: I will learn to hear what you truly need, even before you have the words. I am listening, with love, beneath every sound.
affirmation.hi: Main woh sunna seekhungi jiski tumhe sach mein zaroorat hai, shabd milne se bhi pehle. Main sun rahi hoon, pyaar se, har aawaz ke neeche.

about.en: Garbh Sanskar affirmations are gentle, spiritually-rooted sentences a mother shares with her baby. The calm and love behind your words begin shaping a peaceful inner world for you both, long before your baby is born.
about.hi: Garbh Sanskar affirmations woh pyaari, aadhyatmik vaakya hain jo ek maa apne baby se kehti hai. Aapke shabdon ke peeche ki shaanti aur pyaar, baby ke janm se bahut pehle hi, aap dono ke liye ek shaant andaruni duniya banane lagte hain.

howToUse.en: Sit quietly, rest a hand on your belly, and read the affirmation slowly — once silently, once aloud. Let yourself believe it for these few moments. A minute is enough, and repeating it at the same time each day makes it a calming ritual.
howToUse.hi: Shaanti se baithein, ek haath pet par rakhein, aur affirmation dheere se padhein — ek baar mann mein, ek baar bolkar. In kuch palon ke liye use sach maan lein. Ek minute kaafi hai, aur ise har din ek hi samay dohraana ise ek shaant ritual bana deta hai.

title.hi: Jab Seene Mein Jalan Aati Hai

content.en: If a burning feeling rises after meals these days, try eating smaller portions more often, and stay sitting upright for a while after eating. Cool milk, a little soaked saunf, or a banana can quietly soothe the burn.
content.hi: Agar in dinon khaane ke baad seene mein jalan uthti hai, to chhoti maatra mein baar-baar khaane ki koshish karein, aur khaane ke baad thodi der seedha baithi rahein. Thanda doodh, thodi bhigi saunf, ya ek kela chupchaap jalan ko shaant kar sakta hai.

remember.en: Small, slow meals are kinder to a growing belly.
remember.hi: Chhote, dheeme khaane ek badhte pet par zyada meharbaan hote hain.

babyLearning.en: growing softly, soothed by the steady beat of your heart
babyLearning.hi: naram tarah se badh raha hai, aapke dil ki sthir dhadkan se shaant

insight.en: A child cannot hear a lesson while they feel unloved. Connect with the heart first, and correction lands far more gently.
insight.hi: Ek bachcha tab tak seekh nahi sun sakta jab tak use anchaaha mehsoos ho. Pehle dil se judein, aur sudhaar kahin zyada narmi se baithta hai.

expanded.en: When a child does something wrong, our instinct is to jump straight to the lesson — to explain, scold or fix. But a child who feels scolded first often stops listening before the lesson even begins. The wisdom passed down through generations is simple: connect before you correct. A calm look, a hand on the shoulder, a quiet 'I see you're upset' opens the door that a sharp word slams shut. Once a child feels safe and seen, their heart softens, and only then can your guidance truly reach them. Connection is not a reward for good behaviour — it is the path to it.
expanded.hi: Jab ek bachcha kuch galat karta hai, hamari aadat hoti hai seedha seekh par koodne ki — samjhaana, daantna ya theek karna. Par jo bachcha pehle daant mehsoos karta hai woh aksar seekh shuru hone se pehle hi sunna band kar deta hai. Pidhiyon se chali aa rahi samajh saadi hai: sudhaarne se pehle judein. Ek shaant nazar, kandhe par ek haath, ek dheema 'main dekh rahi hoon ki tum pareshaan ho' woh darwaaza khol deta hai jise ek tez shabd band kar deta hai. Ek baar jab bachcha surakshit aur dekha hua mehsoos karta hai, uska dil naram ho jaata hai, aur tabhi aapka margdarshan sach mein un tak pohanchta hai. Judaav achhe vyavhaar ka inaam nahi hai — yeh us tak pohanchne ka raasta hai.

deepDive.en: Neuroscientists Dan Siegel and Tina Payne Bryson popularised 'connect and redirect': soothing a child's emotional brain first allows the thinking brain to engage. Correction without connection rarely reaches a child who feels alone.
deepDive.hi: Neuroscientists Dan Siegel aur Tina Payne Bryson ne 'connect and redirect' ko mashhoor kiya: pehle bachche ke bhaavnaatmak dimaag ko shaant karna sochne wale dimaag ko jodne deta hai. Bina judaav ke sudhaar shaayad hi us bachche tak pohanchta hai jo akela mehsoos karta hai.

remember.en: Connect first; the correction will follow.
remember.hi: Pehle judein; sudhaar baad mein aa jayega.

summary.en: A wise prince answers a spirit's riddles with patience and truth.
summary.hi: Ek buddhimaan rajkumar dhairya aur sach se ek aatma ki paheliyon ka jawaab deta hai.

body.en:
Long ago, in their years of exile, the five Pandava brothers grew thirsty in a great forest. One by one, four of them went to a still lake to drink, ignoring a voice that warned them to first answer its questions. Each drank, and each fell into a deep sleep beside the water.

At last the eldest, Yudhishthira, arrived. When the same voice — a yaksha, a forest spirit — called out, he did not rush. He bowed and agreed to answer first. The yaksha asked many riddles. 'What is the greatest wonder in the world?' Yudhishthira replied gently, 'That every day people die, yet those who remain believe they will live forever.'

Pleased with his calm and honest answers, the yaksha revived all four brothers. Yudhishthira had learned that patience and truth open doors that haste and pride keep firmly shut.
body.hi:
Bahut samay pehle, apne vanvaas ke saalon mein, paanch Pandava bhai ek bade jungle mein pyaase ho gaye. Ek-ek karke, unme se chaar paani peene ek shaant jheel par gaye, ek aawaz ko anasuna karte hue jisne unhe pehle uske prashn ka jawaab dene ki chetawni di. Har ek ne piya, aur har ek paani ke paas gehri neend mein gir gaya.

Aakhir mein sabse bade, Yudhishthira, pohanche. Jab wahi aawaz — ek yaksha, ek van ki aatma — ne pukaara, woh jaldbaazi nahi ki. Usne jhuk kar pehle jawaab dene par sehmati di. Yaksha ne kayi paheliyaan poochi. 'Duniya ka sabse bada ascharya kya hai?' Yudhishthira ne narmi se jawaab diya, 'Ki har din log marte hain, phir bhi jo bache rehte hain woh maante hain ki woh hamesha jeeyenge.'

Uske shaant aur sachche jawaabon se khush, yaksha ne chaaron bhaiyon ko jeevit kar diya. Yudhishthira ne seekha ki dhairya aur sach woh darwaaze kholte hain jinhe jaldbaazi aur ghamand kas kar band rakhte hain.

title.en: Tell your baby that asking for help is a kind of strength — that the brave are the ones who reach out.
title.hi: Apne baby ko bataayein ki madad maangna ek tarah ki taakat hai — ki bahaadur woh hote hain jo haath badhaate hain.

motivation.en: A child who can ask for help will never have to struggle entirely alone.
motivation.hi: Jo bachcha madad maang sakta hai use kabhi bilkul akele sangharsh nahi karna padega.

description.en: Night raga for a settled, peaceful close
description.hi: Raat ka raag, ek thami hui, shaant samaapti ke liye

affirmation.en: As this night melody deepens, my whole being settles into stillness, holding you in quiet peace.
affirmation.hi: Jaise yeh raat ki dhun gehri hoti hai, mera poora vajood thirta mein bas jaata hai, tumhe shaant sukoon mein thaame.

about.en: Ragas are classical Indian melodies, each tied to a mood and time of day. From about week 18 your baby can hear; even now, the calm a raga brings you surrounds your baby and gently shapes a peaceful environment around your pregnancy.
about.hi: Raag classical Bharatiya dhunein hain, har ek kisi mood aur din ke samay se judi. Lagbhag 18ve hafte se aapka baby sun sakta hai; abhi bhi, raag se aane wali shaanti aapke baby ko gher leti hai aur aapki pregnancy ke around ek shaant mahaul banati hai.

howToUse.en: Play the raga softly while you sit or rest with a hand on your belly. You needn't focus hard — let the sound fill the room as you breathe slowly. Two to five minutes, ideally at the raga's time of day, makes it a ritual your baby will one day recognise.
howToUse.hi: Raag dheere se chalaayein jab aap baithein ya pet par haath rakh kar aaram karein. Zyada dhyan lagane ki zaroorat nahi — aawaz ko kamre mein bhar jaane dein jab aap dheere saans lein. Do se paanch minute, ho sake to raag ke samay par, ise ek aisa ritual bana deta hai jise aapka baby ek din pehchaanega.

content.en: Rest both hands low on your belly. Breathe in slowly and feel it rise gently under your palms, then let it fall as you breathe out. Stay with five soft, rounded breaths.
content.hi: Dono haath apne pet ke neeche rakhein. Dheere se saans lein aur use apni hatheliyon ke neeche pyaar se uthte mehsoos karein, phir saans chhodte hue girne dein. Paanch naram, gol saanson ke saath rukein.

remember.en: Belly breathing tells your body it is safe.
remember.hi: Pet se saans lena aapke sharir ko batata hai ki woh surakshit hai.

babyLearning.en: growing day by day, settling into gentle rhythms of rest and stir
babyLearning.hi: din-ba-din badh raha hai, aaram aur halchal ke naram taal mein bas raha hai

title.hi: Saza Khatam Hoti Hai, Sikhaana Reh Jaata Hai

insight.en: Punishment stops a behaviour in the moment, but teaching changes it for life. One ends; the other lasts.
insight.hi: Saza us pal vyavhaar rok deti hai, par sikhaana use jeevan bhar ke liye badal deta hai. Ek khatam hoti hai; doosra reh jaata hai.

expanded.en: Punishment can feel effective because it works fast — a sharp word, and the misbehaviour stops. But it teaches mostly fear, and fear fades the moment no one is watching. Teaching is slower, yet it plants something that grows. When you explain why kindness matters, show a better way, or let a child feel the natural result of a choice, you give them an inner compass they will carry long after you are gone. The goal is not a child who behaves because they are afraid, but one who chooses well because they understand. Choose the lesson that lasts.
expanded.hi: Saza asardaar lag sakti hai kyunki yeh tezi se kaam karti hai — ek tez shabd, aur galti ruk jaati hai. Par yeh zyadatar dar sikhaati hai, aur dar usi pal mit jaata hai jab koi dekh nahi raha hota. Sikhaana dheema hai, phir bhi yeh kuch aisa bota hai jo badhta hai. Jab aap samjhaati hain ki dayaalta kyun maayne rakhti hai, ek behtar raasta dikhaati hain, ya bachche ko ek faisle ka swaabhaavik nateeja mehsoos karne deti hain, to aap unhe ek andaruni compass deti hain jise woh aapke jaane ke baad bhi lambe samay tak rakhenge. Lakshya woh bachcha nahi jo darte hue achha vyavhaar kare, balki woh jo achha chune kyunki woh samajhta hai. Woh seekh chunein jo reh jaaye.

deepDive.en: Long-term studies find that harsh punishment predicts more defiance over time, not less, while reasoning and guidance build genuine self-control. Children internalise values they understand, not rules they merely fear.
deepDive.hi: Lambe samay ke studies paate hain ki kathor saza samay ke saath aur avgya bataati hai, kam nahi, jabki samjhaana aur margdarshan sachcha aatm-niyantran banate hain. Bachche un moolyon ko andar utaarte hain jinhe woh samajhte hain, un niyamon ko nahi jinse woh bas darte hain.

remember.en: Teach the lesson that outlasts the moment.
remember.hi: Woh seekh dein jo us pal se aage tak rahe.

summary.en: A teacher learns that true skill is born of full, quiet attention.
summary.hi: Ek guru seekhta hai ki sachi kushalta poore, shaant dhyan se janmti hai.

body.en:
In a forest school long ago, a wise teacher gathered his students for a test in archery. He hung a wooden bird high in a distant tree and placed an arrow in each student's hands. One by one he called them to take aim at the bird's eye.

Before each shot, he asked a single question: 'What do you see?' The first student said, 'I see the tree, the branches, the sky, and the bird.' The teacher quietly told him to lower his bow. Another said, 'I see the garden and the bird upon the bough.' He too was asked to step back.

At last came the steadiest student. 'What do you see?' the teacher asked. 'I see only the eye of the bird,' he answered, 'nothing else at all.' 'Now,' smiled the teacher, 'you may shoot.' His arrow flew true. The lesson stayed with them all their lives: it is full attention, not force, that hits the mark.
body.hi:
Bahut samay pehle ek van ke vidyalay mein, ek buddhimaan guru ne apne shishyon ko dhanurvidya ki pareeksha ke liye ikattha kiya. Usne ek lakdi ka pakshi door ek ped par ooonchaai par tanga aur har shishya ke haath mein ek teer rakha. Ek-ek karke usne unhe pakshi ki aankh par nishaana lagane bulaaya.

Har teer chalane se pehle, usne ek hi prashn poocha: 'Tumhe kya dikhta hai?' Pehle shishya ne kaha, 'Mujhe ped, tehniyaan, aasmaan, aur pakshi dikhta hai.' Guru ne shaanti se use apna dhanush neeche karne ko kaha. Doosre ne kaha, 'Mujhe baag aur tehni par baitha pakshi dikhta hai.' Use bhi peeche hatne ko kaha gaya.

Aakhir mein sabse sthir shishya aaya. 'Tumhe kya dikhta hai?' guru ne poocha. 'Mujhe sirf pakshi ki aankh dikhti hai,' usne jawaab diya, 'aur kuch bhi nahi.' 'Ab,' guru muskuraaya, 'tum teer chala sakte ho.' Uska teer sach mein nishaane par laga. Yeh seekh unke saath jeevan bhar rahi: nishaana poora dhyan lagaata hai, taakat nahi.

title.en: Tell your baby that being kind always matters more than winning — that a gentle heart is the real prize.
title.hi: Apne baby ko bataayein ki dayaalu hona hamesha jeetne se zyada maayne rakhta hai — ki ek naram dil hi asli inaam hai.

motivation.en: A child who values kindness over winning grows into someone the world feels safe around.
motivation.hi: Jo bachcha jeet se zyada dayaalta ko mahatva deta hai woh aisa insaan banta hai jiske aas-paas duniya surakshit mehsoos karti hai.

title.hi: Do Ke Liye Ek Shaant Baag

description.en: A short meditation to rest in calm together
description.hi: Saath shaanti mein aaram karne ka ek chhota dhyan

affirmation.en: In this quiet garden, there is room for both of us to simply rest and grow.
affirmation.hi: Is shaant baag mein, hum dono ke liye bas aaram karne aur badhne ki jagah hai.

introduction.en: Settle comfortably and let your eyes close. Take three slow breaths. Picture a calm green garden, warm and still, where you and your baby sit together. With each breath, let the quiet of this garden settle a little deeper into you both.
introduction.hi: Aaram se baithein aur aankhein band hone dein. Teen dheemi saansein lein. Ek shaant hara baag sochein, garm aur thira, jahaan aap aur aapka baby saath baithe hain. Har saans ke saath, is baag ki shaanti ko aap dono mein thoda aur gehraai se basne dein.

closingThought.en: You can return to this quiet garden any time you wish, and your baby will always be there with you.
closingThought.hi: Aap is shaant baag mein jab chahein laut sakti hain, aur aapka baby hamesha wahaan aapke saath hoga.

about.en: Garbh Sanskar meditations are short, gentle practices to connect with your baby — not generic mindfulness, but a few minutes of sending warmth and love inward. Your calm becomes your baby's calm.
about.hi: Garbh Sanskar meditations chhote, pyaare abhyaas hain apne baby se judne ke liye — koi aam mindfulness nahi, balki kuch minute garmjoshi aur pyaar andar bhejne ke. Aapki shaanti aapke baby ki shaanti ban jaati hai.

howToUse.en: Find a quiet spot, sit comfortably and close your eyes. Follow the introduction, breathe slowly, and rest your attention gently on your baby. Two to three minutes is plenty. Doing it daily, ideally at the same hour, deepens the bond.
howToUse.hi: Ek shaant jagah chunein, aaram se baithein aur aankhein band karein. Introduction follow karein, dheere se saans lein, aur apna dhyan pyaar se apne baby par tikaayein. Do se teen minute kaafi hai. Ise rozana, ho sake to ek hi samay karne se bond gehra hota hai.

content.en: If you feel more tired or dizzy than usual, your body may be asking for more iron now. Pair iron-rich foods like spinach, dates, jaggery or lentils with a little lemon or amla — the vitamin C helps your body soak it up.
content.hi: Agar aap aam se zyada thaki ya chakkar mehsoos karti hain, to aapka sharir abhi aur iron maang raha ho sakta hai. Iron se bharpoor cheezein jaise palak, khajoor, gud ya daal ko thode nimbu ya amla ke saath lein — vitamin C aapke sharir ko ise sokhne mein madad karta hai.

remember.en: A little vitamin C helps iron do its quiet work.
remember.hi: Thoda vitamin C iron ko uska shaant kaam karne mein madad karta hai.

babyLearning.en: growing well, stretching and turning in its cosy space
babyLearning.hi: achhe se badh raha hai, apni aaramdaayak jagah mein khinchta aur ghoomta

insight.en: A spilled drink, a forgotten task, a clumsy fall — these are mistakes, not misbehaviour. Learning to tell them apart will change how you parent.
insight.hi: Gira hua drink, bhoola hua kaam, ek phoohad gir jaana — yeh galtiyaan hain, sharaarat nahi. Inhe alag pehchaanna seekhna badal dega ki aap kaise parenting karti hain.

expanded.en: Much of what looks like 'bad behaviour' in a child is simply being small in a big world. A toddler does not knock over a glass to defy you; their hands are still learning. A child who forgets a promise has not betrayed you; their memory is still growing. When we treat honest mistakes as misbehaviour, children learn to hide and fear instead of to try and learn. But when we say 'that's okay, mistakes happen, let's clean it up,' we keep the door to honesty open. Save your firmness for the rare moments that truly need it, and meet the everyday stumbles with warmth.
expanded.hi: Ek bachche mein jo 'bura vyavhaar' lagta hai, uska bahut sa bas ek badi duniya mein chhota hona hai. Ek nanha bachcha aapki avgya mein glass nahi giraata; uske haath abhi seekh rahe hote hain. Jo bachcha vaada bhoolta hai usne aapko dhokha nahi diya; uski yaadaasht abhi badh rahi hai. Jab hum imaandaar galtiyon ko sharaarat maante hain, bachche koshish karne aur seekhne ke bajaaye chhupna aur darna seekhte hain. Par jab hum kehte hain 'koi baat nahi, galtiyaan hoti hain, chalo saaf karte hain,' to hum imaandaari ka darwaaza khula rakhte hain. Apni sakhti un durlabh palon ke liye bachaayein jinhe sach mein zaroorat ho, aur rozana ke ladkhadaane ko garmjoshi se milein.

deepDive.en: Developmental psychologists distinguish 'capability' errors from intentional defiance. Most childhood missteps are simply skills still forming — punishing them teaches shame, not competence.
deepDive.hi: Developmental psychologists 'kshamta' ki galtiyon ko jaanboojh kar ki gayi avgya se alag karte hain. Bachpan ki zyadatar galtiyaan bas abhi ban rahe kaushal hain — unhe saza dena sharm sikhaata hai, kushalta nahi.

remember.en: Not every stumble is a misbehaviour.

title.en: Tenali Rama And The Boastful Pundit

summary.en: A witty courtier humbles a scholar who valued show over wisdom.
summary.hi: Ek hazirjawab darbaari ek vidwaan ko jhuka deta hai jo gyaan se zyada dikhaawe ko mahatva deta tha.

body.en:
A famous pundit arrived at King Krishnadevaraya's court, boasting that no one alive could match his learning. He recited long verses, used the grandest words, and looked down his nose at everyone around him. The courtiers grew tired of his airs, but none dared challenge so learned a man.

Then the king's clever jester, Tenali Rama, stepped forward carrying a huge bundle of palm-leaf books. 'Great pundit,' he said with a bow, 'these are the books my late guru asked me to return to whoever could simply read out their titles.' The proud pundit opened the bundle — only to find every single leaf completely blank.

The court burst into laughter. 'You see,' Tenali Rama smiled, 'true wisdom does not need to announce itself. The emptiest pot makes the loudest noise.' The pundit, red-faced, left his boasting behind.
body.hi:
Ek mashhoor pundit Raja Krishnadevaraya ke darbaar mein aaya, sheykhi maarta ki koi jeevit vyakti uske gyaan ki barabari nahi kar sakta. Usne lambe shlok sunaaye, sabse bhaari shabd istemaal kiye, aur aas-paas sab par naak-bhaun chadhaayi. Darbaari uske naaz-nakhron se thak gaye, par koi itne vidwaan vyakti ko chunauti dene ki himmat nahi kar paaya.

Tabhi raja ka chalaak vidushak, Tenali Rama, taad-patra ki kitaabon ka ek bada gattha leke aage aaya. 'Mahaan pundit,' usne jhuk kar kaha, 'yeh woh kitaabein hain jo mere swargvaasi guru ne mujhe us vyakti ko lautaane ko kaha tha jo bas inke sheershak padh sake.' Ghamandi pundit ne gattha khola — par har patte ko bilkul khaali paaya.

Darbaar thahake maar kar hans pada. 'Dekha,' Tenali Rama muskuraaya, 'sache gyaan ko khud ka elaan nahi karna padta. Sabse khaali ghada sabse zyada shor karta hai.' Pundit, sharm se laal, apni sheykhi peeche chhod gaya.

title.en: Tell your baby that whatever they feel is always allowed — that no feeling will ever be too much for you.
title.hi: Apne baby ko bataayein ki woh jo bhi mehsoos karte hain woh hamesha theek hai — ki koi bhaavna kabhi aapke liye bahut zyada nahi hogi.

motivation.en: A child whose feelings are welcomed never has to hide who they are.
motivation.hi: Jis bachche ki bhaavnaon ka swagat hota hai use kabhi yeh chhupana nahi padta ki woh kaun hai.

title.en: Your Feelings Are Safe With Me
title.hi: Tumhari Bhaavnaayein Mere Saath Surakshit Hain

affirmation.en: Whatever you feel, you may bring it to me. There is no feeling too big or too small for my love to hold.
affirmation.hi: Tum jo bhi mehsoos karo, use mere paas la sakte ho. Koi bhaavna itni badi ya itni chhoti nahi jise mera pyaar thaam na sake.

about.en: Garbh Sanskar affirmations are gentle, spiritually-rooted sentences a mother shares with her baby. The calm and love behind your words begin shaping a peaceful inner world for you both, long before your baby is born.
about.hi: Garbh Sanskar affirmations woh pyaari, aadhyatmik vaakya hain jo ek maa apne baby se kehti hai. Aapke shabdon ke peeche ki shaanti aur pyaar, baby ke janm se bahut pehle hi, aap dono ke liye ek shaant andaruni duniya banane lagte hain.

howToUse.en: Sit quietly, rest a hand on your belly, and read the affirmation slowly — once silently, once aloud. Let yourself believe it for these few moments. A minute is enough, and repeating it at the same time each day makes it a calming ritual.
howToUse.hi: Shaanti se baithein, ek haath pet par rakhein, aur affirmation dheere se padhein — ek baar mann mein, ek baar bolkar. In kuch palon ke liye use sach maan lein. Ek minute kaafi hai, aur ise har din ek hi samay dohraana ise ek shaant ritual bana deta hai.

content.en: I will offer myself the same gentleness I want to give my child — letting my own feelings be felt, without judging them.
content.hi: Main khud ko wahi narmi dungi jo main apne bachche ko dena chahti hoon — apni bhaavnaon ko bina judge kiye mehsoos hone dena.

remember.en: Be as gentle with your feelings as you'll be with theirs.
remember.hi: Apni bhaavnaon ke saath utni hi naram rahein jitni aap unki ke saath rahengi.

babyLearning.en: growing steadily, calmed by a soft and gentle voice
babyLearning.hi: lagaataar badh raha hai, ek naram aur pyaari aawaz se shaant

insight.en: A lesson given in anger is rarely remembered. A lesson given in calm is the one that truly stays.
insight.hi: Gusse mein di gayi seekh shaayad hi yaad rehti hai. Shaanti mein di gayi seekh hi sach mein tikti hai.

expanded.en: When we correct a child while we are angry, the loudness drowns out the lesson. The child remembers the storm — the raised voice, the tense face — but not the wisdom we were trying to share. Calm is what carries a message safely across. A steady voice and a soft face tell a child, 'you are safe, and I am teaching you, not attacking you.' This does not mean you must never feel anger; it means letting the heat pass before you teach. Wait for your own calm, then speak. The lesson that arrives in stillness is the one your child will keep.
expanded.hi: Jab hum gusse mein ek bachche ko sudhaarte hain, aawaz ka shor seekh ko daba deta hai. Bachcha toofan ko yaad rakhta hai — oonchi aawaz, tana hua chehra — par woh samajh nahi jo hum baantna chahte the. Shaanti hi woh hai jo ek sandesh ko surakshit paar le jaati hai. Ek sthir aawaz aur ek naram chehra bachche ko batata hai, 'tum surakshit ho, aur main tumhe sikhaa rahi hoon, tum par hamla nahi kar rahi.' Iska matlab yeh nahi ki aapko kabhi gussa na aaye; iska matlab hai sikhaane se pehle garmi ko guzar jaane dena. Apni shaanti ka intezaar karein, phir bolein. Jo seekh thirta mein aati hai usi ko aapka bachcha rakhega.

deepDive.en: When a child senses anger, their stress response switches on and the learning parts of the brain go quiet — so almost nothing taught in that moment is retained. Calm in the adult literally keeps the child's mind open to learning.
deepDive.hi: Jab ek bachcha gussa bhaanp leta hai, uska stress response chaalu ho jaata hai aur dimaag ke seekhne wale hisse shaant ho jaate hain — to us pal sikhaaya gaya lagbhag kuch bhi yaad nahi rehta. Bade mein shaanti sach mein bachche ke mann ko seekhne ke liye khula rakhti hai.

remember.en: Wait for calm, then teach — that lesson lasts.
remember.hi: Shaanti ka intezaar karein, phir sikhaayein — woh seekh tikti hai.

summary.en: Two teachers show that gentleness shapes a child more deeply than fear.
summary.hi: Do guru dikhaate hain ki narmi ek bachche ko dar se kahin gehraai se gadhti hai.

body.en:
In a village there once lived two teachers, each with a class of young students. The first teacher believed in a sharp tongue and a quick scolding. His pupils sat straight and silent, but their eyes were always lowered, and the moment he left the room, all their learning seemed to scatter like startled birds.

The second teacher spoke softly, even when a child stumbled. 'Try again,' he would smile, 'this is how we learn.' His pupils made mistakes freely, asked endless questions, and slowly grew bold and clever.

Years later, the villagers noticed something. The first teacher's students had learned to obey, but the second teacher's students had learned to think. 'Fear may make a child quiet,' an old grandmother observed, 'but only kindness makes a child grow. Both teachers were heard — but only one was truly remembered.'
body.hi:
Ek gaon mein kabhi do guru rehte the, har ek ke paas nanhe shishyon ki ek kaksha thi. Pehla guru tez zubaan aur jhatpat daant mein vishwaas rakhta tha. Uske shishya seedhe aur chup baithte the, par unki aankhein hamesha jhuki rehti, aur jaise hi woh kamre se nikalta, unki saari seekh dari hui chidiyon ki tarah bikhar jaati.

Doosra guru naram bolta tha, tab bhi jab koi bachcha ladkhadata. 'Phir se koshish karo,' woh muskuraata, 'isi tarah hum seekhte hain.' Uske shishya khulkar galtiyaan karte, anginat sawaal poochte, aur dheere-dheere bahaadur aur samajhdaar bante.

Saalon baad, gaon waalon ne kuch dekha. Pehle guru ke shishyon ne maan-na seekha tha, par doosre guru ke shishyon ne sochna seekha tha. 'Dar ek bachche ko chup bana sakta hai,' ek boodhi dadi ne kaha, 'par sirf dayaalta ek bachche ko badhne deti hai. Dono guru sune gaye — par sirf ek sach mein yaad rakha gaya.'

title.en: Tell your baby that they do not have to be perfect — that they are loved exactly as they are.
title.hi: Apne baby ko bataayein ki unhe perfect nahi hona — ki woh bilkul waise hi pyaare hain jaise woh hain.

motivation.en: A child freed from perfection is free to enjoy simply being themselves.
motivation.hi: Perfection se aazaad bachcha bas khud hone ka aanand lene ke liye aazaad hota hai.

description.en: Night raga for a soft, restful close
description.hi: Raat ka raag, ek naram, aaramdaayak samaapti ke liye

affirmation.en: As this gentle night melody flows, my heart grows soft and light, wrapping you in calm.
affirmation.hi: Jaise yeh naram raat ki dhun behti hai, mera dil naram aur halka hota hai, tumhe shaanti mein lapet kar.

about.en: Ragas are classical Indian melodies, each tied to a mood and time of day. From about week 18 your baby can hear; even now, the calm a raga brings you surrounds your baby and gently shapes a peaceful environment around your pregnancy.
about.hi: Raag classical Bharatiya dhunein hain, har ek kisi mood aur din ke samay se judi. Lagbhag 18ve hafte se aapka baby sun sakta hai; abhi bhi, raag se aane wali shaanti aapke baby ko gher leti hai aur aapki pregnancy ke around ek shaant mahaul banati hai.

howToUse.en: Play the raga softly while you sit or rest with a hand on your belly. You needn't focus hard — let the sound fill the room as you breathe slowly. Two to five minutes, ideally at the raga's time of day, makes it a ritual your baby will one day recognise.
howToUse.hi: Raag dheere se chalaayein jab aap baithein ya pet par haath rakh kar aaram karein. Zyada dhyan lagane ki zaroorat nahi — aawaz ko kamre mein bhar jaane dein jab aap dheere saans lein. Do se paanch minute, ho sake to raag ke samay par, ise ek aisa ritual bana deta hai jise aapka baby ek din pehchaanega.

content.en: If digestion feels sluggish these days, gently add more fibre and water. Soaked raisins, a ripe papaya, warm dal, and plenty of water through the day can keep things moving without any strain.
content.hi: Agar in dinon pachan susta lage, to pyaar se aur fibre aur paani jodein. Bhige kishmish, ek pakka papita, garm daal, aur din bhar khoob paani bina kisi zor ke cheezein chalti rakhte hain.

remember.en: Fibre and water are the gentlest helpers here.
remember.hi: Fibre aur paani yahaan sabse naram madadgaar hain.

babyLearning.en: closing the week, growing surer and stronger each day
babyLearning.hi: hafta poora karte hue, har din aur sthir aur mazboot hote hue

insight.en: When a child does wrong, the goal is to repair the moment, not to make them feel small. Shame closes a heart; repair opens it.
insight.hi: Jab ek bachcha galat karta hai, lakshya us pal ko sudhaarna hai, unhe chhota mehsoos karaana nahi. Sharm ek dil band kar deti hai; sudhaar use khol deta hai.

expanded.en: There is a quiet but powerful difference between guilt and shame. Guilt says 'I did something bad' — and it can be repaired. Shame says 'I am bad' — and it only wounds. When a child makes a mistake, we can guide them to put it right: to say sorry, to clean up, to try again. That is repair, and it teaches responsibility while keeping their dignity whole. But shaming — name-calling, harsh ridicule, withdrawing love — teaches a child only that they are unworthy. Always aim your words at the action, never at the child. Let them know that doing wrong does not make them any less loved.
expanded.hi: Guilt aur sharm ke beech ek shaant par shaktishaali antar hai. Guilt kehta hai 'maine kuch bura kiya' — aur ise sudhaara ja sakta hai. Sharm kehti hai 'main bura hoon' — aur yeh sirf ghaav deti hai. Jab ek bachcha galti karta hai, hum unhe ise theek karne ki raah dikhaa sakte hain: sorry kehna, saaf karna, phir se koshish karna. Yeh sudhaar hai, aur yeh zimmedaari sikhaata hai jabki unki garima poori rakhta hai. Par sharminda karna — naam pukaarna, kathor mazaak, pyaar wapas le lena — bachche ko sirf yeh sikhaata hai ki woh layak nahi. Apne shabd hamesha kaam par lakshya karein, kabhi bachche par nahi. Unhe bataayein ki galat karna unhe zara bhi kam pyaara nahi banata.

deepDive.en: Researchers find that guilt motivates children to make amends, while shame drives hiding and defensiveness. Repair-focused guidance builds responsibility without harming a child's core sense of worth.
deepDive.hi: Researchers paate hain ki guilt bachchon ko galti sudhaarne ke liye prerit karta hai, jabki sharm chhupne aur raksha-bhaav ki taraf le jaati hai. Sudhaar par kendrit margdarshan bachche ki apni keemat ko nuksaan pohanchaaye bina zimmedaari banata hai.

remember.en: Aim your words at the act, never at the child.
remember.hi: Apne shabd kaam par lakshya karein, kabhi bachche par nahi.

title.en: The Gardener And The Young Sapling

summary.en: A patient gardener shows that growing takes gentle time, not force.
summary.hi: Ek dhairyavaan maali dikhaata hai ki badhne mein naram samay lagta hai, zor nahi.

body.en:
A young gardener planted a tiny sapling and longed to see it grow tall and strong. Each morning he hurried out to check it, but the little tree seemed to take its time. Impatient, he tried pulling gently at its stem to stretch it taller, but the sapling only drooped and wilted at his tugging.

An old gardener saw this and smiled kindly. 'You cannot pull a tree into growing,' he said. 'Give it water, give it sun, give it patience — and let it grow at its own pace. Hurry only harms it.'

The young gardener listened. He stopped pulling and simply tended the sapling each day with care and time. Season by season, with no forcing at all, the little tree rose into a strong and shady giant. Some things, the gardener learned, can only be grown gently — and a child is one of them.
body.hi:
Ek jawaan maali ne ek nanha paudha lagaaya aur use lamba aur mazboot badhte dekhne ko taras gaya. Har subah woh use dekhne daudta, par nanha ped apna samay lene laga. Bechain ho kar, usne use lamba khinchne ke liye uske tane ko pyaar se kheenchne ki koshish ki, par paudha uske kheenchne par bas jhuk gaya aur murjha gaya.

Ek boodhe maali ne yeh dekha aur dayaalta se muskuraaya. 'Tum kisi ped ko kheench kar nahi badhaa sakte,' usne kaha. 'Use paani do, dhoop do, dhairya do — aur use apni raftaar se badhne do. Jaldbaazi bas use nuksaan pohanchaati hai.'

Jawaan maali ne suna. Usne kheenchna band kiya aur bas roz dekhbhaal aur samay se paudhe ka khayal rakha. Mausam-dar-mausam, bina kisi zor ke, nanha ped ek mazboot aur chhaaydaar vishaal ban gaya. Kuch cheezein, maali ne seekha, sirf narmi se badhaayi ja sakti hain — aur ek bachcha unhi mein se ek hai.

title.en: Tell your baby that you will always believe in them — even on the days they may stop believing in themselves.
title.hi: Apne baby ko bataayein ki aap hamesha unpar vishwaas karengi — un dinon bhi jab woh khud par vishwaas karna chhod sakte hain.

motivation.en: Carrying a child's belief for them, until they can hold it themselves, is one of love's quietest gifts.
motivation.hi: Ek bachche ka vishwaas unke liye thaame rakhna, jab tak woh khud na thaam sakein, pyaar ke sabse shaant tohfon mein se ek hai.

description.en: A short meditation to close the week in warmth
description.hi: Hafte ko garmjoshi mein samaapt karne ka ek chhota dhyan

affirmation.en: I hold you gently in a warm circle of light, and there you are wholly safe and loved.
affirmation.hi: Main tumhe roshni ke ek garm ghere mein pyaar se thaamti hoon, aur wahaan tum poori tarah surakshit aur pyaare ho.

introduction.en: Sit or lie back comfortably and let your eyes close. Take three slow breaths. Imagine a soft circle of warm light all around you, and picture your baby resting safely at its centre. With each breath, let that light grow a little gentler and a little brighter around you both.
introduction.hi: Aaram se baithein ya letein aur aankhein band hone dein. Teen dheemi saansein lein. Apne charon ore garm roshni ka ek naram ghera sochein, aur apne baby ko uske beech surakshit aaram karte hue dekhein. Har saans ke saath, us roshni ko aap dono ke charon ore thoda aur naram aur thoda aur roshan hone dein.

closingThought.en: This warm light has always been yours to give. Carry a little of it into the days ahead.
closingThought.hi: Yeh garm roshni hamesha aapki dene ke liye rahi hai. Iska thoda sa aane wale dinon mein saath le jaayein.

about.en: Garbh Sanskar meditations are short, gentle practices to connect with your baby — not generic mindfulness, but a few minutes of sending warmth and love inward. Your calm becomes your baby's calm.
about.hi: Garbh Sanskar meditations chhote, pyaare abhyaas hain apne baby se judne ke liye — koi aam mindfulness nahi, balki kuch minute garmjoshi aur pyaar andar bhejne ke. Aapki shaanti aapke baby ki shaanti ban jaati hai.

howToUse.en: Find a quiet spot, sit comfortably and close your eyes. Follow the introduction, breathe slowly, and rest your attention gently on your baby. Two to three minutes is plenty. Doing it daily, ideally at the same hour, deepens the bond.
howToUse.hi: Ek shaant jagah chunein, aaram se baithein aur aankhein band karein. Introduction follow karein, dheere se saans lein, aur apna dhyan pyaar se apne baby par tikaayein. Do se teen minute kaafi hai. Ise rozana, ho sake to ek hi samay karne se bond gehra hota hai.

title.hi: Hafte Ko Saans Ke Saath Chhodein

content.en: Take a long breath in through your nose, then breathe out slowly through your mouth as if gently letting go of the whole week. Repeat four times, letting your body grow a little lighter with each one.
content.hi: Apni naak se ek lambi saans lein, phir apne munh se dheere saans chhodein jaise poore hafte ko pyaar se jaane de rahi hon. Chaar baar dohraayein, har baar apne sharir ko thoda halka hone dete hue.

remember.en: Each long breath out lets a little weight go.
remember.hi: Har lambi saans baahar thoda bojh jaane deti hai.
